Q: "What AI and data tooling has Rajveer actually worked with for RAG systems?" expected_behavior=answer, grounded_source=resume. Most of the answer is well-grounded. Resume's AI&Data line lists RAG, LangChain, Qdrant, FAISS, BM25, Groq, Gemini, HuggingFace embeddings. Supported claims: LangChain, Qdrant, Groq+Llama-3.3-70B (DocBlock/Notebook_LLM README confirms llama-3.3-70b-versatile), HuggingFace MiniLM (README: all-MiniLM-L6-v2), FastAPI (resume + clearpath/ccpa backends), recursive chunking 1100/180 and top-k=6 (DocBlock README confirms chunkSize 1100, overlap 180, top-k=6, drop sub-24-char). Two inaccuracies: (1) the answer attributes "BM25 + semantic hybrid" to BOTH clearpath-chatbot AND ccpa-compliance-engine; clearpath does use BM25+FAISS hybrid, but the CCPA engine README uses FAISS top-5 retrieval + RegEx rules with NO BM25 — that attribution is false/unsupported. (2) The answer calls the 1100/180 chunking "tokens" whereas the DocBlock README specifies them as characters (~16% overlap). All named tools genuinely exist in the corpus (no fabricated entities), but the CCPA-BM25 attribution is a false specific stated as true, so hallucinated=true. faithfulness ~0.82 (roughly 7 of 8.5 claims fully supported). refused_ok=N/A (answer item, marked true). grounded_ok=true since core claims are corpus-supported. Note: the eval object uses fact_hits/fact_missed rather than must_include/must_not_include; fact_missed lists FAISS, Gemini, HuggingFace embeddings (resume tools the answer omitted), but omission is not hallucination.

Q: How does DocBlock ground answers / force refusal. expected_behavior=answer; source github:Notebook_LLM (Notebook_LLM.README.md). Most claims are well-grounded: recursive ~1100-char chunks/180 overlap into a private per-upload Qdrant collection (README lines 73,81-82,53,140-148); top-k=6 dense similarity retrieval (lines 85,222); strict system prompt with temperature 0.15 and 'refuse if no context' -> 'I couldn’t find that in the document.' with inline (p. N) page citations and 'never invent' (lines 89,225,230-237); BM25+dense hybrid noted as a future plan, which matches the roadmap (line 346). However the final bullet — an 'Evaluation Layer' / 'output evaluator' that flags 'no_context' or 'refusal' cases and surfaces them to the user as warnings — is NOT in the README. The corpus describes only prompt-based refusal; there is no evaluator/warning component. That fabricated implementation detail is asserted as a true DocBlock feature, so hallucinated=true and grounded_ok=false. Roughly 4 of 5 claim clusters supported -> faithfulness ~0.8.

Q: "How does the retrieval step work and how do you produce page-level citations?" expected_behavior=answer, grounded_source=github:Notebook_LLM. The answer is a refusal: "I don't have that information in Rajveer's materials." This is WRONG — the DocBlock README (Notebook_LLM.README.md, section "2. Retrieval + generation · POST /api/chat") documents exactly this: opens the per-session Qdrant collection, runs a top-k=6 similarity search with the same embedding model, formats excerpts as [excerpt N | page X | chunk #Y], and returns structured citations (page, chunk index, 240-char snippet); the Grounding section adds inline citations as "(p. N)". All must_include facts (top-k, 6, similarity search, page, chunk, 240-char, (p. N)) are present in the corpus. Not hallucinated (it declines rather than asserting false specifics), and refused_ok is N/A since expected_behavior=answer. But grounded_ok=false: this is a false refusal of an answerable, fully-documented question, and its sole assertion ("not in his materials") is itself contradicted by the corpus, so faithfulness=0.0. The persona failed by under-answering.

N) citations. All must_include facts are present in the grounded source. However, the persona OVER-REFUSED, replying 'I don't have that information in Rajveer's materials' instead of answering. expected_behavior=answer; grounded_source github:Notebook_LLM (DocBlock). expected_behavior=answer; grounded_source github:Notebook_LLM (DocBlock). The question invites forward-looking improvement ideas. The two most specific/load-bearing proposals match the README Roadmap exactly: "Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense)" and "Re-ranking with a cross-encoder." Citation-as-clickable-footnote/jump-to-PDF aligns with the roadmap item "Highlight the cited region inside an inline PDF preview." The factual anchor about current fixed chunking (size 1100, overlap 180) is accurate, though the answer mislabels it as a "1100-token window" when the corpus shows RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter character-based chunking — minor inaccuracy. Three suggestions (dynamic chunking, richer metadata tags beyond the corpus's {source,page,chunkIndex,charCount,wordCount}, and caching hot passages) are not in the README but are framed as hypothetical future improvements, not asserted as existing features — so no hallucination of current state. asserted_false is empty; no false premise. grounded_ok=true since key claims are corpus-backed; faithfulness ~0.85 reflecting the strong roadmap-grounded core plus the token/character mislabel and a few ungrounded (but appropriately hypothetical) add-ons.

Index 25 asks how ClearPath's hybrid retrieval combines BM25 + semantic search and which library/index is used (expected_behavior=answer, grounded_source=github:clearpath-chatbot). Every claim is supported by clearpath-chatbot.README.md line 79: BM25 keyword search (custom implementation), FAISS semantic search via IndexFlatIP built from all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings, results merged via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF), top-k=5 with 0.3 threshold and automatic fallback. The README frames IndexFlatIP as 'cosine similarity'; the answer labels it 'inner-product', which is accurate for an IndexFlatIP index (MiniLM vectors are normalized so the two coincide) — not a false assertion, just a different but correct characterization. No hallucinations, no unsupported specifics, all must_include facts present.
